If the property can be animated or constrained @SINCE_1_0.0.

A valid property index is zero or greater. @SINCE_1_0.0.
A valid handle to the target object.
The index of a property provided by object.
@DEPRECATED_2_4.42 Index of a property sub component, for use with Vector2, Vector3 and Vector4, -1 if using main property
-1 is not a valid property index
-1 is not a valid property key
-1 is not a valid property index

detaileddescription.para:
A move constructor enables the resources owned by an r-value object to be moved into an l-value without copying. @SINCE_1_4.17
other
The Array to move from
After the other array is used, it becomes invalid and is no longer usable.

@SINCE_1_0.0

index

The a reference to the element

@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The element index to access. No bounds checking is performed

@SINCE_1_0.0
index

@SINCE_1_4.17
other
The array to copy from
The moved array.
After the other array is used, it becomes invalid and is no longer usable.

@SINCE_2_3.54
rhs
A reference for comparison
True if equal type and equal value.

@SINCE_2_3.54
rhs
A reference for comparison
True if not equal

briefdescription.para: Get hash value of the array.

detaileddescription.para:
The order of key/value pairs is considered. For example, Array({1, 2}) and Array({2, 1}) might not have same hash value. And the type of Property::Value is considered. For example, Array({1}) and Array({1.0f}) might not have same hash value.
Hash might spend O(N) per each call.
Hash don't consider floating point precision. So even if two values equality return true, they can have different hash value. Property::Arrayv1({1.0f}}); Property::Arrayv2({1.0000001192092896f});//1.0f+FLT_EPSILON assert(v1==v2);//true assert(v1.GetHash()==v2.GetHash());//false,becauseoffloatingpointprecisionissue.
@SINCE_2_3.54
Retrieves the Impl object. @

parameterdescription.para: The string key as a const char *
A string key to compare against.

simplesect.para: true if the key compares, or false if it isn't equal or of the wrong type

parameterdescription.para: The string key as a const char *

simplesect.para: true if the key compares, or false if it isn't equal or of the wrong type

parameterdescription.para: An index key to compare against.

simplesect.para: true if the key compares, or false if it isn't equal or of the wrong type
true if the keys are of the same type and have the same value

parameterdescription.para: A string key to compare against.

simplesect.para: true if the key is not equal or not a string key

parameterdescription.para: A const char* key to compare against.

simplesect.para: true if the key is not equal or not a string key

parameterdescription.para: An index key to compare against.

simplesect.para: true if the key is not equal, or not an index key
true if the keys are not of the same type or are not equal

briefdescription.para: Merges values from the map 'from' to the current.

detaileddescription.para: Any values in 'from' will overwrite the values in the current map.

briefdescription.para: Const operator to access element with the specified string key.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
key
The key whose value to access
The value for the element with the specified key, if key doesn't exist, then Property::NONE is returned
Will assert if invalid-key is given.

briefdescription.para: Operator to access the element with the specified string key.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
key
The key whose value to access
A reference to the value for the element with the specified key
If an element with the key does not exist, then it is created.

briefdescription.para: Const operator to access element with the specified index key.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_1.39
key
The key whose value to access
The value for the element with the specified key, if key doesn't exist, then Property::NONE is returned
Will assert if invalid-key is given.

briefdescription.para: Operator to access the element with the specified index key.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_1.39
key
The key whose value to access
A reference to the value for the element with the specified key
If an element with the key does not exist, then it is created.

@SINCE_1_0.0
other
The map to copy from
The copied map

@SINCE_1_4.17
other
The map to move from
The moved map
The other array is an r-value so becomes invalid and is no longer usable.

@SINCE_2_3.54
rhs
A reference for comparison
True if equal type and equal value.

@SINCE_2_3.54
rhs
A reference for comparison
True if not equal

briefdescription.para: Get hash value of the map.

detaileddescription.para:
The order of key/value pairs is not considered. For example, Map({{"key", 1}, {"key2", 2}}) and Map({{"key2", 2}, {"key", 1}}) will have same hash value. But the type of Property::Value is considered. For example, Map({{"key", 1}}) and Map({{"key", 1.0f}}) might not have same hash value.
Hash might spend O(N) per each call.
Hash don't consider floating point precision. So even if two values equality return true, they can have different hash value. Property::Mapv1({{1,1.0f}}); Property::Mapv2({{1,1.0000001192092896f}});//1.0f+FLT_EPSILON assert(v1==v2);//true assert(v1.GetHash()==v2.GetHash());//false,becauseoffloatingpointprecisionissue.
@SINCE_2_3.54
Retrieves the Impl object. @
This creates a property with type Property::NONE. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Explicitly sets a type and initialize it.

detaileddescription.para:
A move constructor enables the resources owned by an rvalue object to be moved into an lvalue without copying. @SINCE_1_4.16
value
The property value to move from

@SINCE_1_0.0
value
The property value to assign from
a reference to this

@SINCE_1_4.16
value
The property value to move from
a reference to this

@SINCE_2_1.31
rhs
A reference for comparison
True if equal type and equal value.

@SINCE_2_1.31
rhs
A reference for comparison
True if not equal
This class is not a base class. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Queries the type of this property value.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The type ID

It will be works only if both input and output are scalar type. - Property::BOOLEAN, Property::FLOAT, Property::INTEGER.
@SINCE_2_3.41
targetType
Target type of the conversion.
True if convert is successful, false otherwise. If the conversion fails, the original value is not modified.
Works on a best-effort approach; if value type is different returns a default value of the type.

@SINCE_1_4.36
A value of type T
Works on a best-effort approach; if value type is different returns a default value of the type. Specialization for enumeration values

@SINCE_1_4.36
A value of type T

@SINCE_1_4.36
enumValue
On return, an enumeration value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is different
GetType() is any enumeration

@SINCE_1_0.0
boolValue
On return, a boolean value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to bool.

@SINCE_1_0.0
floatValue
On return, a floating-point value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to float.

@SINCE_1_0.0
integerValue
On return, an integer value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to int.

@SINCE_1_0.0
rect
On return, an integer rectangle
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to Rect<int>.

@SINCE_1_0.0
vectorValue
On return, a vector value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to Vector2.

@SINCE_1_0.0
vectorValue
On return, a vector value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to Vector3.

@SINCE_1_0.0
vectorValue
On return, a vector value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to Vector4.

@SINCE_1_0.0
matrixValue
On return, a matrix3 value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to Matrix3.

@SINCE_1_0.0
matrixValue
On return, a matrix value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to Matrix.

@SINCE_1_0.0
angleAxisValue
On return, a angle-axis value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to AngleAxis.

@SINCE_1_0.0
quaternionValue
On return, a quaternion value
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to Quaternion.

@SINCE_1_0.0
stringValue
A string
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() is a type convertible to string.

@SINCE_1_0.0
arrayValue
The array as a vector Property Values
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() returns Property::ARRAY.

@SINCE_1_0.0
mapValue
The map as vector of string and Property Value pairs
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible
GetType() returns Property::MAP.

extentsValue
Extents, a collection of 4 int16_t
true if the value is successfully retrieved, false if the type is not convertible

briefdescription.para: Get hash value of the value.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
object
A valid handle to the target object
propertyIndex
The index of a property

@SINCE_1_0.0 @DEPRECATED_2_4.42
object
A valid handle to the target object
propertyIndex
The index of a property
componentIndex
Index to a sub component of a property, for use with Vector2, Vector3 and Vector4. -1 for main property (default is -1)

@SINCE_1_0.0
object
A valid handle to the target object
propertyName
The property name
This performs a property index query and is therefore slower than constructing a Property directly with the index.

@SINCE_1_0.0 @DEPRECATED_2_4.42
object
A valid handle to the target object
propertyName
The property name
componentIndex
Index to a sub component of a property, for use with Vector2, Vector3 and Vector4. -1 for main property (default is -1)
This performs a property index query and is therefore slower than constructing a Property directly with the index.

briefdescription.para: Non-virtual destructor; Property is not intended as a base class. @SINCE_1_0.0.

detaileddescription.para: An object + property pair. @SINCE_1_0.0.
A value-type representing a property value. @SINCE_1_0.0.
A Map of property values, the key type could be String or Property::Index. @SINCE_1_0.0.
A key type which can be either a Dali::String or a Property::Index @SINCE_1_2.7.
